entry.title: Error: SFC Step is not in active status
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are doing start and complete of an operation activity in order POD with a production process.

When we try to complete the operation activity we are getting an Error showing as -

“ {"errorCode":"HTTP400","errorMessage":"{\"error\":{\"message\":\"SFC step ID is not in active status.\",\"causeMessage\":null,\"code\":\"sfc.step.status.not.active\",\"correlationId\":\"a31614f8-fbba-401a-8e3d-4e1965d28e52\",\"arguments\":[\"StatusBO:9990,405\"]}}"} ”

 

We see the status changed to green when we start the operation activity in PPD as shown below.

PPD start the operation-

But when we try to complete the Operation activity with complete operation activity, it is throwing the error as – SFC Step ID is not in active status.

 

Could you please explain this error, why this issue is coming when we are using the inbuilt standard plugins.

Thanks in advance.

KR,
Say

entry.title: Re: Is Seamless Planning between SAC and SAP Datasphere a...
entry.content: Thank you for your response @Alecsandra

entry.title: Re: Partner Consolidation Unit is blank in ACDOCA
entry.content: Did you check if the assignment between company code and company is complete for these three units? You can check in T-Code OX16

entry.title: Re: Use different exchange rates between B/S and P&L
entry.content:
Hi,

With regards to currency translation to a group presentation currency in accounting, we recommend the following:

  • Accounting
    • have an alternative measure in accounting capturing the group presentation currency (e.g. global currency)
    • Currency translation in accounting happens while posting. 
    • The closing rate cannot be available at that point in time
    • The translation in accounting allows you to have an accurate translation at the spot rate of the transaction for P&L and BS movements, which is the closest possible to get to accounting standards like IFRS. It also allows you to get a preliminary view on your group currency amounts at any time reflecting the latest accounting journals
  • Group Reporting
    • Do closing rate translation at closing rate in Group Reporting as part of your closing activities
    • integrate the original accounting group currency together with the local (functional) currency (e.g. release task)
    • run currency translation task task in GR
    • Result is that you have 2 group currency amounts. The original one translated at the spot rate of the transaction which is in line with the one in accounting, and the closing rate (e.g. for BS only).
    • A difference is calculated and stored on a separate translation key as well as on a dedicated subitem (transaction type) or in the case of equity on a separate FS item (currency translation reserve).

Hope this helps.

Kind regards,

Florian

entry.title: Re: Maintenance plan was not scheduled - Error
entry.content: Hi Memem,

entry.title: Re: Canada ECP- Cross-year retro payroll and tax for Cana...
entry.content:
Hello Woody,

Thank you for reviewing the scenario and providing your inputs. Standard calculation for Canada payroll has an option to bring forward the differences from prior year but the earnings are treated as non-periodic income and negative values are not taxed. Even if I change to regular taxable income and add to current pay period income (PP01/2025) to calculate the correct taxes, because of the 2 weeks lag for absences, next period (PP02/2025) will retro into PP01/2025 and reduce the income again if employee is on an unpaid absence. The taxable income for PP01/2025 goes negative and system does not calculate tax on negative income. So the refund of taxes is only for the original amount that was calculated and pay statement does not reflect the correct tax when compared to the earnings. Hope this explains the scenario in detail.

Thanks again and appreciate the inputs!

entry.title: Fetch user details (country) from IAS in the approuter
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community, 

I am working on a Cloud Foundry Approuter in SAP BTP and have the following custom middleware code:

 

const approuter = require('@sap/approuter');

const ar = approuter();

ar.beforeRequestHandler.use('/', function (req, res, next) {
    if (req.url.includes('/sap/opu/odata/MY_SERVICE/')) {
        req.headers.email_user = req.user.name;
        console.log("username");
        console.log(req.user.name);
        console.log("headers");
        console.log(req.headers);
        console.log("req");
        console.log(req);
    }
    
    next();
});

ar.start();

 

 

In this setup, I am trying to retrieve the country of the authenticated user. I know the user's email is accessible via req.user.name and I have also found the JWT Access Token in the request. However, the token does not include any region or country information.

Since country information is maintained in SAP IAS (Identity Authentication Service), I’m wondering:

  • Is it possible to retrieve the country of the user from the req object or its associated JWT token?
  • If not, what would be the recommended approach to fetch country information from IAS in an SAP BTP App Router application?

Any pointers or advice would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance!
Mohammed

entry.title: Re: Maintenance plan was not scheduled - Error

Hi @memem 

Before counter - 240 reaches it cycle. We are able to do manual call. After entering counter - 240 value as 2100, We are unable to see the calls in IP10. IP10 screen will appear like below image.

entry.title: Capturing Delta Requirements: A Guide to Fit-to-Standard Workshops
entry.content:
You are about to transform your business by replacing or upgrading your 15- or 20-year-old Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) platform. With a 'Cloud First' approach, you have presented the project's key objectives to management and chosen the SAP Business Suite as your future ERP. Your sandbox environment is set up with the Enterprise Management Layer and best practices. Now, you are ready for transformation. What's next?

Fit-to-Standard workshops

As per the SAP Activate methodology, a few tasks happened during the explore phase.

  1. Define business architecture <blog>
  2. Perform a fit-to-standard analysis to capture Delta requirements (backlog) using SAP Signavio Process Manager 

Points to note for process design during Fit-to-standard workshops

  • Workshops are driven by business process owners who are transformation champions and complimented by strong IT SMEs
    • This is an opportunity for the IT team to understand what outcome the business wants to achieve
  • Consider all relevant applications and steps to complete a business process
    • Don’t just focus on SAP-relevant steps. Include other system steps/interfaces, and don’t forget to capture manual steps
  • Create a structure of ‘Business Process Hierarchy’ before you begin workshops <blog>
    • The hierarchy will help you schedule workshops and assign subject matter experts
    • Hierarchy will help you to summarize/aggregate the findings at various levels

Capturing Delta Requirements in Fit-to-Standard Workshops

Fit-to-standard workshops are structured around Business Capabilities (focused on business or functional areas) or End-to-End Business Processes (focused on processes). These workshops explore functionality, demonstrate how solutions and best practices meet business requirements, and enable business process experts to define ‘to-be’ processes.

Delta requirements are identified, discussed, and then added to the backlog. The backlog consists of documented requirements, improvements, or functionalities required to complete a process. These items are prioritized, iteratively reviewed, and refined.

Understanding Business Process

Once you have defined the Business Process Hierarchy, you have a list of business activities.

Anatomy of a Business Activity (modular business process):

  • is a granular business process that creates value for internal or external stakeholders.
  • will have consistent naming conventions
    • will have a unique process flow that is defined using a ‘BPMN’ diagram
    • will have a clear start and end
    • will have a unique sequence of a task (level 5) to be performed. Each task is divided into steps (level 6)
    • Tasks (L5) will be performed by a role/agent. They will utilize application/s where data is used/analyzed and output/value is created. Policies may exist, and the organization may control business rules.
    • Each task (L5) will be broken down into steps (L6). These steps are performed in a given application or used by an interface. Steps will include required business logic/rules.

Understanding requirement collection

SAP provides standard solution flow diagrams and best practice diagrams. During the fit-to-standard workshop, delta requirements were collected at multiple levels. The following diagram shows the level at which the delta reequipments are captured.

Figure 1 - Capturing Detla requirements (backlog)

Process focused

1. Confirm whether a process is required at a process level.

This may require a scope discussion with the project team

  • Examples:
    • Create a purchase order for services > Required
    • Creating sales orders for services > Not required

2. What other process variants exist

Caution: This is a slippery slope for future enhancements. More variants will result in more business logic and business roles, especially when different business units require variances to execute the same processes

  • Examples:
    • Create a purchase order for the project purchase
    • Create a purchase order for the stock purchase
    • Create purchase orders for services based on contract

Application focused

3. Business rules: (expression of policy)

  • Examples:
    • Purchasing Approvals above a certain threshold
    • Applying tax codes and calculation

4. Business logic (expected system behaviours):

  • Examples:
    • Status updates for an outline agreement
    • Budget control checks

5. Enhancement/extension requirement

  • Examples:
    • A field on the PO is required to indicate ‘recycled material.’
    • Derive material handling requirements for dangerous goods
  • If business rules and business logic can not be implemented by application configuration, then additional extensions and enhancements are required.

Tip: Always ask clarifying questions while defining business logic/business rules to minimize enhancement/extension. Implementing legacy logic (or illogic)  without rationalizing will create technical debt.

6. Interface requirement

  • Example:
    • Send purchase orders to a specific vendor via an electronic interface

Data/Information focused

7. Data requirement

  • Example:
    • Enterprise Structure: Company codes, Plants, Sale Org
    • Master data: Material, Vendor, cost center etc.
    • Transaction data: Purchase orders, Purchase requisition
    • Various data requirements can be captured here, e.g. Data quality, data retention, data validation

8. Reporting requirement

  • Example:
    • Report to see purchases from a vendor in a given time (for ATIP)

9. Output/Forms

  • Example:
    • PO printouts (including PDF) as per Canada’s procurement guideline

User role/Security focused

10. Automation:

  • Example:
    • Auto approval for PO/IR less than a certain amount
  • Tip: Processes designed with clear business rules, business logic and data structure are prime for automation 

11. Security/Access control

  • Examples:
    • The purchasing officer can not enter goods receipt or invoice receipt
    • Mask credit card numbers

Rules of engagement during Fit-to-Standard (F2S) exercise.

  • This exercise simplifies and streamlines processes by minimizing variances, establishing clear business rules and logic, and documenting precise requirements to achieve desired outcomes. Avoid documenting requirements as ‘as-is’. The status quo is not your friend.
  • Convoluted and contradictory rules/logic will result in messy processes with unnecessary coding that must be maintained for a long time.
  • Simplify processes and data structures. This will positively impact user experience and reduce your application management costs for years.
  • Note that specific enhancements and integrations are inevitable if they must meet legislative requirements or interface with other existing systems. Focus on re/designing these extensions in a compliant manner
  • Once these requirements are reviewed, refined, and approved, SAP SMEs can work on developing process design documents and creating specs for configuration, enhancements, extension, and integration. Additional requirements, such as data conversion and non-functional requirements, can be captured at a higher level.
  • Use SAP Signavio Process Manager attributes to capture requirements and notes. Define requirement categorization, priorities, dependencies, etc. Capture dependencies
  • Don’t squander this is an opportunity to get close to the user; consider this as a ‘design thinking’ exercise.

Conclusion:

While consultants and the project team will eventually move on to other projects, the users will continue to execute these processes for the foreseeable future. It's crucial to design and implement the best processes possible, as users are relying upon you to enhance their efficiency and effectiveness. Your thoughtful design will have a lasting impact on their daily operations

Users are counting on you to bring out their best!

entry.title: Chart Display Issues with SAP BW Live Queries in SAC
entry.content:
Dear Community,

We are encountering an issue while using an SAP BW Live Query in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) during our migration process from Lumira Designer to SAC.
Our goal is to reuse the same BW queries in SAC that were previously implemented in Lumira Designer. However, we are facing problems with the display of a chart widget.

Here’s the situation:

  • The result in Lumira Designer (and also in Analysis for Office) displays correctly, as expected by our customer:

Lumira:

 Analysis for Office

 

 The query behind this result is quite complex and involves cell definitions. Despite this complexity, both Lumira Designer and AFO manage to display the charts as required.

In SAC, however, the chart displays incorrectly:

While I can manually adjust the colors to group the measures so they visually align with Lumira Designer and AFO, the legend remains incorrect:

 

  • The query output consists of filtered measures (5 measures = 5 columns) in the rows.
  • The columns contain a complex structure, including time selections and version information (e.g., ACT P011/2024).

 Since the query is highly complex, adapting or simplifying it is not an option.

My key question is:
Are there known limitations or workarounds in SAC for handling complex BW queries with such structures?

Any guidance or insights would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Mario

entry.title: SAP FSM Work Time visibility on Dispatching Board
entry.content:
Dear Community, 

since the 2411 Release Work Time can be seen in the T&M Journal.

Is it possible to somehow not display work time on the dispatching board that is created by technicians since you can see it in the T&M Journal anyway?

Best regards,
Yanik

entry.title: SAP Datasphere Replication Flows
entry.content:
Good day,

I am encountering issues with replication flows on my test Datasphere tenant. Last year, I successfully installed and activated the Finance Foundation business content, and the transactional data replication flows were functioning as expected.

However, I recently attempted to run the same replication flow and encountered the following errors. When I deployed the replication flow I was presented with the date incompatibility error below:

And then presented with the following error when I ran the replication flow and it failed:

 

The Datasphere instance is connected to an S/4HANA On-Premise system (Release 2021, SP1).

Has anyone else experienced a similar issue or know how this can be resolved?

Thank you for your assistance.

Best regards,

Sim S

SAP Datasphere

entry.title: Celebrating the Latest Public Cloud Release for Product Compliance
entry.content:
Today marks an exciting milestone with the latest Public Cloud release for Product Compliance. This update brings new features, updated content, and improved processes, all designed to enhance user experience and streamline compliance management.

For those dealing with dangerous goods, staying compliant is crucial. The latest release includes new compliance requirement versions for the Australian Dangerous Goods Code (ADG) and the International Air Transport Association Dangerous Goods Regulations (IATA-DGR). For detailed instructions on connecting to our Regulatory Content Service, see the SAP Note 2999140. Another note on dangerous goods content, there is also a help page dedicated to all things content here.  A side note about the content service, it can be set up to deliver new compliance requirement versions, but you still need to activate the new version for it to be used in the system.

Packing instructions according to dangerous goods regulations data is also visible in the View Regulatory Data - Dangerous Goods app. Packing instructions according to dangerous goods regulation data shows the acceptable single and combination packagings. For combination packagings, the acceptable inner packagings, outer packagings and when applicable, the maximum permitted quantity in each inner or outer packaging are shown per packing group. This information is also part of the Dangerous Goods List for Packing Instructions.

  • For ADR, packing instructions for the following types of packages have been added:
    • Packages other than IBCs: P001, P002, P003, P004, P004, P005, P006, P099, P206, P207, P401, P402, P403, P404, P410, P502, P503, P907, P908, P909, P911
    • Intermediate bulk containers: IBC520, IBC620, IBC99
    • Large Packaging: LP02, LP03, LP101, LP102, LP200, LP 621, LP622, LP902, LP903, LP904, LP905, LP906, LP99

 

Here you can see the View Regulatory Data – Dangerous Goods app, and now there is a clickable link within the Enclosure Instruction Code. This link takes you to additional packaging information, structured like the actual dangerous goods regulations.

This is regulation dependent, and while this is not available for regulations, it is something we will continue to expand as data becomes available.

Moving on to product marketability, there are a lot of updated compliance requirement versions, and you can see complete list with additional information here. You can also see a complete list of available compliance requirement for product marketability here.

  • AU AIIC Status (Jul 2024)
  • CA DSL+NDSL Status (Jun 2024)
  • CA Drug Precursors (Jun 2024)
  • CH Chemical Risk Reduction Ordinance Status (Jun 2024)
  • CN RoHS (Jun 2024)
  • CN IECSC Status (May 2024)
  • EU Drug Precursors (May 2024)
  • EU ODS Status (Feb 2024)
  • EU REACH SVHC Status (Jun 2024)
  • EU Drug Precursors (May 2024)
  • EU REACH Annex XVII Status (May 2024)
  • JP ENCS Status (Jun 2024)
  • JP PDSCL Status (May 2024)
  • JP ISHA Status (Mar 2024 Rev.1)
  • JP CSCL Class 1 and 2 Status (Jul 2024)
  • JP CSCL Exempt Status (Jun 2024)
  • JP CSCL General Chemicals (Jul 2024)
  • MY Poisons List (May 2024)
  • NL ZZS List Status (May 2024)
  • RS Banned + Restricted Substances Status (Apr 2024)
  • SG Drug Precursors (May 2024)
  • US TSCA Export Notification (Jun 2024)
  • US TSCA Inventory Status (May 2024)
  • US TSCA SNUR Status (Jun 2024)
  • World IEC 62474 DSL (Jan 2024)
  • World PFAS Screening List Status (Apr 2024)

For safety data sheet management, authoring and occupational exposure limits there are 2 new compliance requirements and 14 new versions. The complete list with additional info can be found here. You can also see a complete list of available compliance requirement for SDS Management here,  SDS Authoring here, and Occupational Exposure Limits here. 

New compliance requirements

  • EU Specific Aerosol Labelling (Nov 2016)
  • EU Waste Code (Dec 2014)

New Versions

  • Bulgaria. Occupational Exposure Limits (Apr 2024)
  • Canada. Occupational Exposure Limits - British Columbia (Apr 2024)
  • China. Occupational Exposure Limits (Jul 2024)
  • Estonia. Occupational Exposure Limits (Apr 2024)
  • Germany. TRGS Occupational Exposure limits (Jun 2024)
  • Korea. Permissible Exposure Levels (Jun 2024)
  • Latvia. Occupational Exposure Limits (Apr 2024)
  • Netherlands. Occupational Exposure Limits (May 2024)
  • Poland. Occupational Exposure Limits (Jul 2024)
  • Romania. Occupational Exposure Limits (Mar 2024)
  • Singapore. Occupational Exposure Limits (May 2024)
  • Slovakia. Occupational Exposure Limits (Jun 2024)
  • Slovenia. Occupational Exposure Limits (Apr 2024)
  • USA. Workplace Environmental Exposure Levels (WEEL) (Jun 2024)

Finally, don't miss out on the SAP EHS and SAP Product Compliance & Stewardship communities in-person in Budapest, where we’ll be exploring the latest SAP S/4HANA innovations. Whether you are planning for future ERP projects, interested in a training opportunity for a member of your team,  or are looking to learn directly from other users, there won’t be another opportunity in 2025 to meet the full SAP ecosystem face-to-face. Click on the banner for more information.

entry.title: Re: Automating Schema Name Management in CI/CD for CAP Ap...

entry.title: Re: Creating Type Group and Using it in ABAP Program
entry.content: Its simple & Superb thank buddy

entry.title: Re: How to post GI on the RF without TM?
entry.content:
Hi @VictorVillarpando ,

it is not possible to post goods issue on RF in Warehouse Management. RF is built for the persona of the Warehouse Operative who performs shopfloor tasks such as picking and packing. The goods issue functionality is available for the Warehouse Clerk, e.g. with the apps:

  • Manage Outbound Delivery Orders
  • Warehouse Monitor
  • Schedule Goods Issue Posting

If you require the function on a mobile RF device you have the options to:

  1. Build a custom app based on the warehousing APIs such as the Warehouse Outbound Delivery Order to post goods issue
  2. Extend the existing RF transactions with Screen Personas as described in this blog, e.g.by adding a new button to an existing screen which will call the API to post goods issue.

Best regards,
Christian

entry.title: Invalid Analytics Model - No mapping attribute found for dimension
entry.content:
Invalid Analytics Model - No mapping attribute found for dimension

This issue is done by the last SAP Datasphere update (09/01/2025 - 22/01/2025) - Note 3560510

1. It is important to have an unchanged copy available or (in production for example) because it is necessary to be able to find the technical dimensions and names used before the update

2. Identify associations with problems, Error messages in the analytical model direct you to the problematic dimensions

Here it is the Time-dependent Equipment dimension which comes from the Time-independent Equipment dimension

The entire branch needs to be redone
3. The original branch must be deleted, in order to be able to rename the new branch. So it's important to have another model available as a reference.
4. Select the Node N-1 of the problem node and you will find in the list of fields the available dimensions (associations)

 

The problematic dimension should be there, but not checked

 

5. Check the dimension and click the arrow on the right to go in. Here, tick 'Use associated dimension'

 

6. Go back a step and change the technical name of the dimension to the original.
This name is also the name of the association in view n-1

 

Technical name adapted in the analytical model:

The technical name of the association in the Equipment (Dimension) view, the original dimension of the problematic dimension:

 

7. Click the pencil next to the attributes and choose 'None' for the technical name. Otherwise the technical names of the fields change back and the reports will no longer find them.

 

It's a major part because this technical names on attribut have an impact on your SAC report ! 

Be careful that if in the past you had defined a suffix or prefix, you must put the same one back (see your AM prod)

 

8. Now you can add the fields of the dimension. Also reference an unchanged template to check if the description has not been changed.

 

9. Repeat for the rest of the branch and for other problematic dimensions.

Note, in Production at the level of SAC reporting, we don't see the impact of the Datasphere update because the AM works on the basis of the deployed version but once you want to redeploy it it will be wrong.

By Jorg Vandersteen

entry.title: Error: 'Template variant cannot be deleted; it has been used to output a document'
entry.content:
When attempting to delete the form template, the system displays the following error message:

'Template variant cannot be deleted; as it has been used in an output document'


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Error: 'Template variant cannot be deleted; it has be...
entry.content:
As per the system's standard behavior, it is not possible to delete a form template once it has been used in an output document. Deleting form templates that are already used in business documents can lead to inconsistency.

Note: This applies to all form templates in the system.

entry.title: Re: How to create picking warehouse tasks for an outbound...
entry.content:
Hi @VictorVillarpando ,

it is not possible to create picking warehouse task on RF in Warehouse Management. RF is built for the persona of the Warehouse Operative who performs shopfloor tasks such as picking and packing. The picking warehouse task creation is available for the Warehouse Clerk, e.g. with the apps:

  • Manage Outbound Delivery Orders
  • Warehouse Monitor
  • Schedule Warehouse Task Creation

If you require the function on a mobile RF device you have the options to:

  1. Build a custom app based on the warehousing APIs such as the Warehouse Order and Task to create picking warehouse task
  2. Extend the existing RF transactions with Screen Personas as described in this blog, e.g.by adding a new button to an existing screen which will call the API to create picking warehouse tasks

Best regards,
Christian

entry.title: Re: Help: the configuration path for the ""Tax Tape"" fie...
entry.content: App "Set Tax Rates - Purchasing", right now it's only have Tax Type for Sales of Argentina. we need to use this field for define tax code with condition table 181

entry.title: Re: Email List - List Tag SAP CPQ

entry.title: How to make a acuired SAC model 24 months rolling
entry.content:
Hi,

I have made a model in SAC which is getting updated every day from a sap hana database.
Now I want to have it in such a way that it always has the current months and past 23 months. But I don't know how to make this. I can filter on the year, such that I only have 2024 and 2025. But I want to have it that I have now feb 2023 to jan 2025 included and next month march 2023 till feb 2025. Is this possible?

Also when I want to add something to a model later on it says:

"The query selection cannot be changed"

I want to change a attribute of a column to textual but this is not allowed. Are you not allowed to change a model once it is made?

SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: How to read archived file in SAP?
entry.content:
Dear Experts

    We want to setup archiving for our CRM ibase tables.

    I want to understand what happens when archiving for SAP tables data using SARA?

    Is the data from the tables first getting copied to a file with name xxxxx.ARCHIVE?

    Then the data is deleted from the respective tables?     

    I have a requirement to read the  xxxxx.ARCHIVE file but its not readable in text editor.

    How to read the archived file and convert that nto excel file?

Thanks

Gopal

entry.title: Request for Solution: Global Compensation View for Fixed and Variable Pay
entry.content:
Hello 

@xavierlegarrec

My client would like to have a comprehensive view of employees' total compensation packages. Specifically, they want to be able to see fixed compensation details while entering variable pay, and vice versa.

Do you have any suggestions to meet this requirement, aside from using the Total Compensation solution?

Thank you in advance for your input and ideas.

Alae.

entry.title: Re: Deactivation of Compliance Requirements in SAP S/4HAN...
entry.content:
Hi @HarryStraussReal, for your situation, yes you need to manually deactivate the old version because the old version needs to first be replaced by the new version in compliance assessments of the compliance view. This can be the case when both are still active because both are still valid. This can sometimes happen, so therefore in order to protect products in different scenarios; the situation is possible. Once the new version is active, all assessments are recalculated and then old version can then safely be deactivated without causing any issues. Here is a short bit from the Help Portal which I think provides additional information.

 

 
As a global product stewardship specialist, you can use the Activate Compliance Requirements – Safety Data Sheet app to:

  • Get a list of the activated compliance requirement versions so you have an overview of which compliance requirement versions are being used.

  • Set an active compliance requirement version to inactive so that it can’t be used anymore in your products.

  • Stay informed if an active compliance requirement version has been updated so you can decide if the relevant safety data sheet assessments have to be recalculated.

  • Get a list of the inactive compliance requirement versions so that you can decide if any of them need to be activated.

  • In case of compliance requirements using the Components with Occupational Exposure Limits or the Hazardous Ingredients compliance patterns, during activation, select your preferred legal area needed to determine the precise composition. The composition is used as base data for propagation, calculation, and substance selection. You can revise the customer-specific regulatory lists that are assigned to the requirement. You can also view the delivered regulatory list assignments to this requirement. Once you've carried out the configuration, you can activate the requirement for use in follow-on business processes.

  • Trigger the recalculation of Occupational Exposure Limits (OEL) assessments that were calculated for a specific compliance requirement version when the version has been changed or corrected.

What Happens with the Entered Data

You activate compliance requirement versions in the Activate Compliance Requirements – Safety Data Sheet app after the content provider or product stewardship specialist for product compliance has created and released them in the Manage Compliance Requirements - Safety Data Sheet app. The product stewardship specialist for product compliance can add active compliance requirements to compliance purposes in the Manage Compliance Purposes app, and activate new compliance purposes. The product stewardship specialist for product compliance can assign active compliance purposes to products, and the master data specialist for product compliance can assign compliance purposes to raw materials.

I hope this helps to clarify, we are working on improving the information in the Help Portal and automating the Content Service more and more to make the entire compliance requirement version activation and deactivation easier.

entry.title: SAP S/4 HANA Cloud - Return order Document flow
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In SAP S/4 Hana Cloud, creating the return order from VA01. After creation of return order, delivery created automatically. after delivery doing the PGR and inspection manually, then In-House repair created automatically. after in-house repair perform precheck repair quotation, repair order,  repair conformation and billing details creates one after another.

so, could you please suggest released view for complete cycle.

Thanks in Advance..!

entry.title: Lowering the SFC quantity In SAP DM
entry.content:
I need to lower the sfc or build quantity, the desired result is the user presses a button on the pod, this changes sfc/build quantity to match the done qty, when the done quantity is hit on this or the next operation. A final confirmation of CNF is received in the ERP and the order is moved out of the pod. This is on a production order in a discrete environment.  

I was able to do this in MII by lowering the build quantity but can't find an api in DM to do this.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: converting binary data of word document to PDF in background
entry.content:
Thanks for the info.

But, could you please provide more details on how you did, because it is bit important for us.

entry.title: Re: Migrating Applications from XSJS to Async-XSJS
entry.content:
Hi Formisan,

BR Volker

entry.title: Staffing Resources with No availability
entry.content:
Hi Team,

In the Staffing Overview app, I am attempting to assign a resource to a task, but when I perform a search, it displays all the resources, including those who have no availability. Additionally, I am able to assign a resource even if their availability is  0, which leads to potential scheduling conflicts.

Is there a way or a setting that can block the assignment of resources with zero availability? Is there way to make the system throw an error or warning message?

Regards,

Gunadharshini

entry.title: Re: Possible to add a new supplier whose master data does...
entry.content:
Hi Raheel,

Thanks for the feedback, however there is a SAP note which states that from new version of S4, One time vendor is not supported.

For reference : 2681606 - Unable to assign a one-time vendor in Manage RFQ

Is there any other simpler way to achieve one time vendor creation as in our business this is a very common scenario. 

Is there any work around?

entry.title: Re: How to send data to AWS S3 when S3 API are not exposed to internet ?
entry.content: Hi Michael, Did you get any solution to you problem. we also want to use S3 bucket using API end point and SAP cloud connector but We find any experiences on internet. It would great help if you could share your experience

entry.title: Re: Deactivation of Compliance Requirements in SAP S/4HAN...
entry.content:
Hi Michael (@Michael_Mulder),
thanks for your feedback.
As the system gave the message that the previous one was deactivated and the previous one was already historical, I thought the dectivation is done by the system 🙂

It is not an issue to deactivate the CRs manually, it is just important to know this (and now I do).

Thanks
Harry

entry.title: What is new in S/4HANA Manufacturing for Production Engineering & Operations (PEO) in 2023 FPS2
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Release 2023 FPS2 for S/4HANA Manufacturing for production engineering and operations (PEO) offers a range of new features and enhancements designed to improve efficiency and flexibility in production engineering and manufacturing processes. This blog post explores the key innovations in the areas of production engineering, production operations, concurrent engineering, and configuration management.

Production Engineering

Logical Components for Unitized Products

In the Manage Shop Floor Routings app for products with unitized parameter effectivity, you can use logical components if you need to assign alternative components to operation activities that are otherwise identical. This can save you time and effort as you don't need to split the operation activity (OA). This results in more efficient shop floor routing maintenance for operation activities that have different components for different effectivity ranges.

So instead of having to create a separate operation activity for each component, as was the case in previous releases:

 

You now only need to create one operation activity with a logical component that acts as a placeholder for the components used in each model-unit effectivity range:

For more information see here: How to Avoid Activity Splits for Alternative Components with Different Effectivity Ranges

Simplification of Production Version for Unitized Products

Handling of the production version is now simplified for products with unitized parameter effectivity to provide you with more transparency. Only one valid production version exists at a time, which means:

  • There is no need to display a Version Details table.
  • When you create a new version of the MBOM and shop floor routing and update the production version, the system overwrites the existing production version with the new one.
  • No valid-from date is required: the production version is always valid from the creation date.

For more information see here: Understanding the Version Details Table

Usability Enhancements for Work Instructions

When you create work instructions in the Manage Shop Floor Routings app, you can now display and change the details of an inline hyperlink reference, which means you can easily identify the object to which the hyperlink refers, and you can also change the display text if you need to add more clarity.

For more information see here : How to Edit References in Work Instructions

You can also automatically generate a table of contents for work instructions to enable seamless navigation between different sections of large-scale work instructions – saving time for both the work instruction author and the production operator. This navigation pane is also available for standard texts.

 

For more information see here: How to Create Bookmarks in Work Instructions

Production Operations

Rework Handling in the Order-Specific Shop Floor Routing

If, as a result of a defect, the quality engineer has inserted a rework operation into the routing structure, it is now possible to change rework operations and operation activities in the order-specific shop floor routing. This means that you can edit operation activities, as long as they have not yet been started in execution.

You can also perform the following activities, if necessary, resulting in greater flexibility in the event of order changes:

  • Assign components to the rework operation activities.
  • Add/remove work instructions, inspection characteristics, PRTs, buyoffs, qualifications, and so on, to/from rework operation activities, as part of a follow-up order change.

For more information see here: Performing Unplanned Rework

Enhanced Serial Number Group Processing

Several new features improve the execution of serial numbers in mass processing mode.

In the serial number group, you can now also include operation activities that contain batch-managed and quantity-based traceable components. This allows the assembly of batch-managed and quantity-based components within one order or across several orders.

When creating a defect for a serial number group, you can now add an attachment to upload pictures, photographs, and documents related to the defect. These attachments are then also available in the Process Defects app where they can be viewed by the production or quality engineer to provide visual support for assessing the situation.

When you complete or skip a serial number group, the system now automatically gives you the option to copy the group so that you can continue to process it at the next available operation activity. This makes the process more efficient when you want to continue processing all serial numbers together.

For more information see here: Process Serial Number Groups

Enhancements to the As-Built Report

Several new features guarantee accurate traceability information and provide capabilities to reconcile the As-Built Report with the actual production state of a product.

You can now add a component to the as-built information that was initially planned in the production order and that has been physically assembled in execution but for which no data collection has been performed. This might happen, for example, if the production operator on the shop floor simply forgot to record the data in the Perform Operation Activity app.

You can also add a component to the as-built information that was never a part of the production order (unplanned), but which has been physically assembled in execution, in order to reflect rework or an ad-hoc replacement, for example.

You can also configure a reason code and note when you add a component. Furthermore, these newly added components are not only added to the as-built information but are also available in the other apps for monitoring production, including the Order Genealogy and Product Genealogy, as well as the Production Action Log providing all the necessary tracking information.

For more information see here: As-Built Report

Printing in PEO

Printing is now possible from a wide variety of apps in PEO. This is supported by Adobe templates, which you can configure to define the format and content of the output according to your requirements.

You can now print operation activities, including their work instructions, from the following four apps:

  • My Work Queue
  • Perform Operation Activity
  • Manage Production Orders
  • Manage Production Operations

For more information see here: How to Print Operation Activities in Manage Production Operations

You can also print product genealogy and order genealogy from the Product Genealogy and Order Genealogy apps.

For more information see here: How to Print the Order Genealogy  

End User Extensibility

In the Perform Operation Activity app, you can use app extensibility to add your own custom columns in the interactive work instructions table. You can also add action buttons for your own custom actions. These features allow you to provide your production operators with exactly the information and capabilities they need.

To make result recording more efficient based on your needs, you can also add additional columns to inspection characteristics using the app extensibility in the Perform Operation Activity app.

For more information see here: App Extensibility: Perform Operation Activity (Expert)

Concurrent Engineering

Handling of BOM Maturity in EBOM-Driven Scenario

BOM maturity levels provide enhanced flexibility and collaboration between product design and production engineering. The production engineer can get a head start with planning activities, such as MRP, even when the product design is not fully complete. In this release, it is now possible to assign maturity levels to EBOMs.

The Change Impact Analysis now detects changes in BOM maturity levels, and you can control how MBOMs inherit the BOM maturity level during the EBOM-to-MBOM handover in the Visual Enterprise Manufacturing Planner (VEMP).

For more information see here: Handling of BOM Maturity Levels  

Configuration Management (Variant Configuration)

Configure-to-Order (CTO): Improved Handling of Object Dependencies in VEMP

A variety of new features in VEMP enable you to manage the impact of changes in variant configuration during the EBOM-to-MBOM handover.

  • It is possible to display information on object dependencies for both the source EBOM and the target MBOM in the source, working, and target panels of VEMP.
  • VEMP adjusts object dependencies if you restructure the target MBOM during the EBOM-to-MBOM handover.
  • You can maintain local object dependencies for BOM items in your target MBOM in the working panel by navigating to the Dependency Maintenance Table (DMT) on the Web UI.
  • Variant configuration in PEO now supports both local and global object dependencies. You cannot create a global object dependency in VEMP, but you can assign an existing global object dependency to an MBOM item.
  • It is possible to reconcile object dependencies by comparing differences between the object dependencies in the source EBOM and the object dependencies in the target MBOM and making decisions on how to handle them.
  • You can implement a BAdI to handle object dependencies in cases where object dependencies exist at multiple levels of the source EBOM hierarchy and where using the Plan-as-Designed function is not a viable option.

For more information see here: Handling of Object Dependencies

Engineer-to-Order (ETO): New App for Managing ETO Production Models

A new app, Manage Production Models – Engineer-to-Order, provides enhanced flexibility when implementing customer-specific requirements. You can use this app to create and maintain engineer-to-order (ETO) production models for configurable materials. That is, you have created and configured a sales order item for a particular customer but the standard configuration options in the sales order do not cover all the customer's requirements. Therefore, you still need to make some additional customer-specific adjustments to the MBOM and shop floor routing to cover this customer's specific needs.

The production model provides a joint view of the manufacturing bill of material (MBOM) and the shop floor routing and offers an alternative way of handling BOM components and shop floor production steps: side-by-side in one application.

  • Based on the 150% configure-to-order production model, you can create an ETO production model (which consists of an ETO version BOM and ETO shop floor routing).
  • You can enhance the ETO production model by adding, removing, or modifying BOM components or production steps, for example.
  • You can use the ETO production model instead of the generic CTO production model to create the production order for a sales order item.

For more information see here: Manage Production Models - Engineer-to-Order

Want to Find Out More?

You can find more information on all new features provided in SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for production engineering and operations (PEO) Release 2023 FPS2 here:

  • What's New | SAP Help Portal – just go to Manufacturing for Production Engineering and Operations

entry.title: Re: How can I call a destination subaccount via CAP
entry.content:
@Emanuele 

Please find the CAP documentation for guidance on how to connect to external services via BTP destinations.

https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/using-services#btp-destinations

entry.title: Customer & Partner Roundtable for SAP BTP ABAP Environment #20
entry.content:
👉 The call for contributions for one of the upcoming roundtables is open! 

 

If you want to show a demo or share a use case scenario for SAP BTP ABAP Environment send us an email and we will get back to you.
 

Introduction

 

As SAP BTP ABAP environment (aka Steampunk) and ABAP Cloud became more popular inside and outside of SAP, there is a high demand for rolling out the latest product news and updates, asking questions, and showing demos. 

💡 You can find the slides presented, recordings, and further references from the previous roundtables in this GitHub repository.


Meeting Information

When:                                                                      
  • February 13th, 10:00 - 11:00 AM CET  – Zoom Meeting (please register in advance) 
 
Who:
  • All interested customers, partners, and stakeholders are invited to join this roundtable
  • BTP ABAP team:
    • Frank Jentsch (Product Lead for SAP BTP ABAP Environment)
    • Uenal Akkaya & Thomas Alexander Ritter (ABAP Cloud Dev Tools)
    • Iwona Hahn, Burcu Karlidag, Ronny Pahlke, Nora Klemp (BTP ABAP Product Management)
Preliminary Agenda:
  • Product update for SAP BTP ABAP Environment
  • ABAP IDE Actions including live demo
  • Q&A 
Looking forward to meeting you!
 


Check out our SAP Business Technology ABAP Environment page in SAP Community for product updates and upcoming events.

entry.title: Re: Partner Consolidation Unit is blank in ACDOCA
entry.content: Hi Chinnapa Reddy Allam

entry.title: Set the value for start date of THISWEEK semantic operator of date field in FilterBar to a Monday
entry.content:
I have an OVP dashboard wherein I have a date field, which is defined as a date range via the underlying ABAP CDS & manifest.json settings.

 

The field displays the from date for the THISWEEK, LASTWEEK & NEXTWEEK semantics as a Sunday which the client wants to be changed to show a Monday instead

I went through the documentation and could only find the inclusion & exclusion of semantics as described in the links below:

Enabling Semantic Operators in the Filter Bar

Configuring the Global Filter

Would appreciate any pointers on how to have the From Date of the THISWEEK, LASTWEEK & NEXTWEEK semantics to get defaulted to a Monday would be greatly appreciated. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Public Cloud TM: prevent freight unit creation for PO...
entry.content:
Hello @Wolfgang_Mayer 

The is the "beauty" of the public cloud. It is "take it or leave it" solution, no custom enhancements. 

I guess you would need to use a dedicated purchasing group. By the way using a purchasing group which is meant to model buyers, to control TM integration is a weird idea in itself. 

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: In PO line, searching material should be restricted based on authorized material group.

I need to understand how should i migrate data for this custom table using LSMW.

Best regards.

kind regards

entry.title: Re: Lock errors using eclipse over the internet
entry.content: Is there any progress? The problem still exists for us. Why does this happen with ADT and not in SE80 via SAP GUI?

entry.title: 2 Transfer Requirement Generated Against Same Process Order
entry.content:
One of my client is facing an issue whenever process order is released system generates 2 Transfer Requirement documents for the Order with different materials and quantities.

Guru's please suggest is there is way to control this, only single Transfer Requirement is needed for each process order

entry.title: Re: Siemens Team Center integration with SAP Signavio
entry.content:
Hello Ayse,

for connecting Siemens Teamcenter and SAP S/4HANA (or SAP ERP), there is a standard integration available, developed jointly by Siemens and SAP (based on our partnership since 2020). It consists of two parts, one from SAP ("PLM System Integration") and one from Siemens ("Teamcenter Gateway for PLM System Integration"). You can find more information on the SAP Help Portal and the Siemens Support Center:

- https://help.sap.com/plmsi_s4hana (look esp. into the Administration Guide!)

- https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_TEAMCENTER_BY_SIEMENS_GATEWAY_PLMSI

- https://docs.sw.siemens.com/en-US/doc/281683587/PL20231124838742656.T4ST_sc.xid1340292/xid1365056 (requires credentials)

If you need more information, please contact me directly!

Best regards,

Thomas Elsässer, SAP SE, Product Management

entry.title: Re: Chemical Vs. Material in EHS S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello Ali, 

To elaborate on your case, the way I understand it:

Your client has a material they want to be able to Risk Assess; this material might be a waste material, which is why they need both master data solutions.

In case my understanding is correct, if the customer needs both, they can assign a waste material to the chemical and further Risk asses the chemical. To do so, one user must have both authorizations for creating and maintaining waste material and Risk asses the chemical. This can be done by separate roles and authorizations depending on the clients' user structure as well. 

Let me know if further guidance is needed, or if this doesn't answer your questions we can get in touch.

Best regards,

Slaveya Petrova

entry.title: Re: Health Monitoring with Cloud ALM - Custom Metrics for S/4 HANA private cloud Part 1 (Postman)
entry.content:
Hi Marcal,
nice blog 🙂
Helped us alot...

Do you have a example to READ-OUT some Metrics?
We know, that we must create a Instance/servicekey with read instead of write,
but we don't know, how the request body should look like 🙂
We tried without Body (We suggest that all metrics are read out from the servideID), we only get an empty result.

We used https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/apis/raw-data-outbound-metrics-api to create it with postman, but still only an empty response.

Kind regards
Christoph

entry.title: Re: SAp Enable Now Producer Connection error
entry.content: Thank you Shane,

entry.title: Re: Condition type EK02 In pricing procedure
entry.content: Hello, we had the same problem and it has been resolved. The source is Standard Price in the material master data. Please check the Standard price field values ​​under Account1 tab in MM02.

entry.title: Re: SAp Enable Now Producer Connection error
entry.content: Actually, I have made the change Dirk put in his post yesterday. It is in the Internet options, need to remove the 1.3 TLS.

entry.title: Re: Outsourced Manufacturing - Classic Subcontracting
entry.content:
Thank you Mohamed!

Very useful document

entry.title: Re: MRP Types P1 to P4
entry.content: Is there any business case for these strategies? When these will be used

entry.title: Re: MRP Types P1 to P4
entry.content:
Similarities 
P1, P2, P3, P4Planned orders will be created outside the PTF and Requirements outside PTF will be always considered for planning
Difference 
P1, P3Requirements inside PTF will be considered for planning
P2, P4Requirements inside PTF will not be considered for planning
P1, P2Planned orders which comes inside PTF will be Firmed automatically
P3, P4Planned orders which comes inside PTF will not be Firmed

entry.title: Material Master Data Mass Maintenance - Delete value from EAN code/EAN category field
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Please kindly advise if it is possible to mass delete value from "EAN/UPC" field and "EAN category" field in material master data in S4HANA Public Cloud system. 

Currently, we need to change the EAN code for around 10k records from having value to blank. We have tried with "Start Mass Maitenance - Products" but it is not going well.

Our firts attempt was clearing the value in "EAN/UPC" and "EAN category" and remove check from "Main EAN"  as in attached screenshot, the process ended with no error but the data did not change.

Our second attempt was to add Action Code D to delete the record from table MEAN but it got error as in below screenshots. 

Thank you in advance!

Regards,

Emi

entry.title: Re: How to post GI on the RF without TM?
entry.content: Thanks, @Christian_Ries ! Best regards

entry.title: Re: How to create picking warehouse tasks for an outbound...
entry.content: Thanks, @Christian_Ries ! Best regards

entry.title: Re: Can I create a 9 grid Performance/Potential story rep...
entry.content:
Hi,

Yes, this is possible. The easiest way is by using a Cross tab table where you have the count of employees as measure and the Performance rating on the columns and the Potential rating on the rows. (makes sure you filter out the null and no ratings)

You can also have you own image of a 9 grid and then add 9 numeric pointer charts. Each chart can then be a count where you filter on each performance / potential rating combination. This can have a negative impact on the performance compared to the cross tab table. 

There probably are also other chart types possible as long as you can put your performance and potential dimensions on an X- and y-axis.

Kind regards,

Jeroen

entry.title: Re: How to refresh the token for a OAuth 2.0 Client via R...
entry.content: Here is a code that worked for me, by bypassing the OAuth2 Config to get the Token, at leas this gives me the token:

entry.title: Could not read working days information from TFACS table - insufficient privilege
entry.content:
Another problem that appeared during the update and was not resolved is the Workdays_between function, this function was the subject of several notes 3396583 and 3300174 which allowed the function to work correctly

 
Since the January update on SAP Datasphere, Analytics models fall into error when fact views that use the function with the following error:
 
The query executed with errors.
Caught exception : exception 70002048: column store error: exception 334060: Could not read working days information from TFACS table "SPACE001"."TFACS_ABC" - insufficient privilege: Detailed info for this error can be found with guid '9A184BAE96589C49AA8F7EFFBCCEF981' at Authorization/insuffPriv/impl/InsufficientPrivilegeThrow.cpp:365
 
It should be noted that this happens if the Fact view is shared and therefore the analytical model is in a different space than the useful TFACS remote table.
 
The view and the tables are shared but without solving the problem

 If the model is created in the same space as the TFACS view/table, the model works correctly.

By Patrice Mathieu

entry.title: Re: How to refresh the token for a OAuth 2.0 Client via R...
entry.content: types: begin of tp_token, access_token type string, end of tp_token. data: l_user type string, l_auth type string, M_TOKEN type STRING , l_url type string, ls_token type tp_token. * Get new token from the authentication server l_url = '[Connection to Token URL]'. cl_http_client=>create_by_url( exporting url = l_url importing client = data(lo_http_client) " HTTP Client Abstraction exceptions argument_not_found = 1 " Communication Parameters (Host or Service) Not Available plugin_not_active = 2 " HTTP/HTTPS communication not available internal_error = 3 " Internal Error (e.g. name too long) others = 4 ). call method lo_http_client->request->set_version exporting version = if_http_request=>co_protocol_version_1_1. * Set up the HTTP Post call method lo_http_client->request->set_method exporting method = 'POST'. lo_http_client->request->set_header_field( name = 'Content-Type' value = 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded' ). l_auth = '[clientSecret]'. l_user = '[clientID]'. * Body of POST Request (SET_CDATA) and Content Length: concatenate 'grant_type=password' '&' 'client_id=' l_user '&' 'client_secret=' l_auth '&' 'username= ' '[username]' '&' 'password=' '[password]' into data(lv_cdata). data(lv_length) = strlen( lv_cdata ). call method lo_http_client->request->if_http_entity~set_cdata exporting data = lv_cdata length = lv_length offset = 0. lo_http_client->send( exceptions http_communication_failure = 1 http_invalid_state = 2 ). lo_http_client->receive( exceptions http_communication_failure = 1 http_invalid_state = 2 http_processing_failed = 3 ). " Read the result and save the token data(lv_result) = lo_http_client->response->get_cdata( ). /ui2/cl_json=>deserialize( exporting json = lv_result " JSON string changing data = ls_token " Data to serialize ). m_token = ls_token-access_token.

entry.title: Employee master upload issues through LSMW
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

We have an issue while uploading Employee master through LSMW tool in SAP S/4HANA.  

We could not progress in uploading from infotype 0009 (Bank Details), 588 (Provident Fund, Income Tax Regime), 587 (ESI), 185 (Personnel ID - Aadhaar, PAN, UAN, Gratuity number).

Could you please advise on how to upload Employee master successfully in SAP S/4HANA using LSMW tool. This requirement is for India specific.

Also, please advise any alternate options available for uploading Employee master like BAPI. 

Please advise. 

Thank you,

kind regards

Thangaraj Thomas

entry.title: Re: Duplicate Records in Story Reports using Performance...
entry.content:
Hi,

It will depend on how you created your query but be aware that when a form is not yet completed then you can get multiple rows because the employee can still be rating, the manager can still be rating and other participant can still be rating. For each participant you will get an additional row. You can work with filters to get the row you would like to see. For example from the manager.

Kind regards,

Jeroen

entry.title: Création de rôles composites dans SAP
entry.content:
En tant qu'administrateur, vous souhaiterez peut-être créer un menu de rôle personnalisé pour certains rôles. Ainsi, les utilisateurs peuvent accéder à leurs transactions ou sites Web fréquemment utilisés en un seul clic. Nous savons tous qu'un utilisateur individuel peut facilement ajouter des transactions ou des sites Web dans le dossier favori de son compte personnel. Comment personnaliser le menu pour un rôle alors ? SAP peut vous aider dans cette situation. Le blog suivant vous montrera comment personnaliser le menu des rôles par étapes.

1.      Tapez PFCG dans le champ de commande de la barre d'outils standard. Vous accédez alors à l'écran suivant.

Entrez le rôle : SINGLE_ROLE_1_E-xxx (vous pouvez trouver votre rôle dans SU01)

 

                   

Cliquez sur le bouton de modification

 

Sélectionnez l' onglet Menu dans l'écran Modifier les rôles

Mettez en surbrillance le dossier « Menu rôle » ( ne cliquez pas sur la flèche – cliquez sur le symbole du dossier juste avant le menu rôle )

Cliquez ensuite sur l’icône du dossier comme indiqué ci-dessous.

 

L'écran Créer un dossier s'affiche. Entrez le nom du dossier : Menu personnalisé

Cliquez

 

 

 

 

Un nouveau dossier nommé Menu personnalisé sera ajouté au menu Rôle comme indiqué ci-dessous : 

 

2.      Créez maintenant un autre dossier nommé Extras sous le menu Personnalisé .

Mettez en surbrillance le dossier « Menu personnalisé » , puis cliquez sur l’icône du dossier comme indiqué à l’étape 1 précédente et créez un nouveau dossier appelé Extras .

L'écran suivant devrait apparaître avec un nouveau dossier Extra sous Menu personnalisé . 

 

3.      Sélectionnez maintenant le menu personnalisé et cliquez sur le bouton Transactions . 

Saisissez le code de transaction VA05 et appuyez sur Entrée. Le texte sera renseigné automatiquement

       Cliquez sur Attribuer des transactions

 

 

      L'écran suivant s'affiche. La liste des transactions de commandes client apparaît sous le dossier Menu personnalisé .

 

 

Vous remarquerez que la transaction a été ajoutée au menu personnalisé.

4. Sélectionnez maintenant le dossier Extras et cliquez sur le bouton Autre 

 

 

      L'écran suivant apparaîtra

 

 

Assurez-vous que l'adresse Web ou le fichier est sélectionné et cliquez sur 

Tapez l'adresse Web souhaitée :

 

 

Cliquez sur Entrée et vous remarquerez qu’une entrée de moteur de recherche a été ajoutée au dossier Extras .

5. Cliquez sur l'onglet Autorisations

 

Cliquez sur  Modifier les données d'autorisation . Et cliquez sur Oui pour enregistrer le rôle.

6. L' écran Modifier le rôle : autorisations apparaît

Cliquez sur le triangle jaune juste avant REPRÉSENTANT COMMERCIAL. 

 

La fenêtre suivante apparaîtra. Acceptez-la en appuyant sur Entrée

 

 

Cliquez sur le bouton Enregistrer , puis sur Générer un profil   dans la barre d'outils standard

 

 

Cliquez sur  Retour, vous remarquerez que tous les onglets, y compris Autorisation et Utilisateur, sont devenus verts.

7. Actualisez le rôle composite pour refléter les modifications apportées aux deux rôles uniques :

Cliquez sur Retour jusqu'à atteindre l' écran Maintenance des rôles

Entrez le rôle : Z_COMPOSITE _ROLE_3_E-XXX (vous pouvez trouver un rôle composite à l'aide de SUIM)

 

8. Cliquez sur le bouton Modifier et cliquez sur l'onglet Menu , puis cliquez sur le bouton de menu Lire

 

Cliquez sur le bouton Réimporter , puis sur Enregistrer. L'onglet Menu de l'écran Modifier les rôles ressemble à l'écran suivant :

entry.title: Re: XML Namespace Error with XPATH condition
entry.content: Hi Snayak, Thanks, it worked! Thanks Maik and Ryan for your valuable response.

entry.title: i have set of gross up wage types net amount it is added to cumulation , but in RT it is not added
entry.content:
Hi community

i have set of gross up wage types 3667, 3668, 3669, 3670 for country CA. net wage type cumulation is marked to /190, but in RT it is not added to /190.

 

Solution: i have updated the processing class 20 with 8 unchanged reference and added to cumulation. it is now working

entry.title: Re: Build Process Automation - Monitoring Tiles to WZ
entry.content: Got it. Its there in Control Tower Environments Public More > Processes and Workflows

entry.title: Re: Advance Workflow in Incentive Management SF configration
entry.content:
Hello @Sayyid ,

1. What do you have in mind with other functionalities? The workflow component is focused on the creating and working with custom workflows and their surrounded processes (like sending out mail notifications to case assignees). 

2. You can trigger API calls from a workflow case with a script. This way you could use the incentive management pipeline API endpoint to start pipelines from workflow

3. There is a Workflow API you can use to work with workflow objects from outside of workflow. You can find the documentation here: Workflow API Documentation

4. As far as I know you need to contac SAP Support for that. It is recommended to work with the IM APIs instead of direct DB Access though (see Accessing Incentive Management Database from Workflow )

entry.title: Re: How to refresh the token for a OAuth 2.0 Client via R...
entry.content: Thanks! This is similar to what we were using 2 years ago, before we switched to using OA2C_CONFIG.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud User and Team Provisioning SCIM API Best Practices and Sample Scripts
entry.content: Well done on the Blog

entry.title: Re: How to refresh the token for a OAuth 2.0 Client via R...
entry.content: But this bypassed OA2C_CONFIG completely, right? Our issue with this back then was a. we would like to use OA2C_CONFIf as it's the "official" SAP way and b. We had to store the client secret, or request it every time.

entry.title: Re: How to refresh the token for a OAuth 2.0 Client via R...
entry.content: Yes this bypasses the OA2C_CONFIG completely, and I agree I wanted to use this standard SAP way for this type of authentication and it looked very promising. However there is a preasure from the client to have this automated, so I will bypass it now and follow the development on a better standard solution from SAP.

entry.title: Re: Print Form Adaptation in Sales/Service 2: Get XML data for preview in Adobe LiveCycle Designer
entry.content:
Hi @sari_irfan,

Sorry, for the late response!

In Version 2, you only need to add the extension field to the service (Settings -> Extensibility Administration --> choose service/business entity --> Custom Fields ). No additional "add to form" tasks. 🙂

After you have added the field to the service, it will be included in the PDF generation automatically (given that it contains a value in the object instance you're creating the PDF for).

Extension fields are located in a separate node of the data json/xml called "extensions".
In an example for the sales quote, I have created a field called "ItemPrintOption". In this case, the binding of a text field in ALD would be: $.extensions.ItemPrintOption

Hope that helps!

Best regards,
Felix

entry.title: Re: Document Information Extraction Templates with SAP Business One
entry.content:
Hello there, 

 

I miss the functionality of excluding certain pages or Positions of a document .

 

kind regards,

Philipp

entry.title: Re: Template Feature of Document Information Extraction
entry.content:
Hello there, 

 

I miss the functionality of excluding certain pages or Positions of a document .

 

kind regards,

Philipp

entry.title: SAP BTP, Kyma runtime: APIRule migration - timelines
entry.content:
Hello everyone! A couple of months ago, I published two blog posts:

  • SAP BTP, Kyma runtime: APIRule migration—noAuth and jwt handlers
  • SAP BTP, Kyma runtime: APIRule migration—ORY Oathkeeper based OAuth2 handlers

The blog posts explain migration details focusing on API. Now, I would like to focus on timelines and important milestones. 

Timeline Overview

Let's quickly recap the events surrounding the migration to the stable v2 version of APIRule. API Gateway 2.7.0, promoted to the regular channel on October 28, 2024, introduced a fully functional APIRule v2alpha1. At the same time, APIRule v1beta1 became deprecated and scheduled for deletion. These two events opened a testing window, allowing you to start testing the API and the migration procedure, report any bugs, and provide feedback. By the end of March 2025, we plan to extend the API Gateway module with stable APIRule v2, and APIRule v2alpha1 will become deprecated. This date also opens a migration window, letting you change your configurations to version v2. In mid-May 2025, APIRule v1beta1 will be deleted, and finally, in mid-June 2025, APIRule v2alpha1 will be deleted. Then, the API Gateway module will only serve stable APIRule v2. 

Milestones

October 28, 2024 - Introduced APIRule v2alpha1 (current state of the art)

The API Gateway module introduced a fully functional APIRule v2alpha1 that, in the future, will replace version v1beta1. The v2alpha1 API and its functionalities are considered stable and are expected to be promoted to v2 with minimal to no changes. APIRule v2alpha1 was introduced to give you the possibility to get familiar with the new API, test it out, and share your feedback. The API Gateway module fully supports migration to version v2alpha1, ensuring zero downtime during the process.  At this point, no immediate action is required. Do not underestimate this change, though, and start planning for the next milestone. We highly encourage you to test the migration process to estimate the required effort accurately and thoroughly understand the changes. Can you already migrate? Yes! We are not discouraging this approach as we fully support APIRule v2alpha1 and provide assistance in case of any issues. You must remember, though, that APIRule v2alpha1 is not the final version and will be removed in June 2025.

APIRule v2alpha1 is not the final version and will be removed in June 2025. Therefore, you must migrate all APIRules to version v2 by that date.

March 31, 2025 - APIRule v2 stable version

That will be the day! APIRule v2 will come to life. On March 31, 2025, APIRule v2 will become the official stable version and the main serving version. This means that when you run the command 'kubectl get apirules -A -o yaml`, APIRule v2 will be displayed by default. To request version v1beta1 or v2alpha2, you must explicitly specify these versions in the commands. For example, you can use `kubectl get apirules.v2alpha2.gateway.kyma-project.io my-rule -o yaml` or `kubectl get apirules.v1beta1.gateway.kyma-project.io  my-rule -o yaml`. This is especially important if you rely on CI/CD tools or use the command-line interface to list deployed resources.

APIRule v2alpha1 and v2 are not fully compatible with v1beta1, so it might happen that v1beta1 rules, such as OAuth2 handlers, won't be fully displayed as v2alpha1 or v2.

You might be wondering: What will happen to my old APIRules? The answer is nothing will change immediately. They will still be there and continue functioning until May 12, 2025. To view them, you must explicitly request the v1beta1 version.

The introduction of v2 will start the migration window. During this period, migrating all APIRules from v1beta1 and v2alpha1 to the newest version, v2, is mandatory. If you are migrating from v1beta1, please refer to my previous blog posts for guidance. If you are migrating from v2alpha1, it should be as simple as changing the version. 

May 12, 2025 - APIRule v1beta1 end of life

At this point, you must have all your APIRules migrated and saved in version v2. APIGateway Controller will stop serving APIRule v1beta1, the version will be removed from CustomResourceDefinition (CRD), and the API Gateway module will delete all unmigrated custom resources (CRs). However, your workloads exposed with APIRule v1beta1 will not be taken down. We will ensure everything keeps running.

Resources created by APIGateway Controller will not be deleted but will become unmanaged. This means you will be responsible for configuring and maintaining them according to your needs. Additionally, Ory Oathkeeper will no longer be managed by the API Gateway module, and if you have APIRules based on it, you will need to take over its management. The migration procedure from unmanaged resources to APIRule v2 will not be supported in an automated way. Therefore, it is very important that you complete the migration to v2 before May 12, 2025, from both versions v1beta1 and v2alpha1.

June 16, 2025 - APIRule v2alpha1 end of life

Finally, v2alpha1 will be removed from the CRD, and all existing CRs will be stored as the v2 version. From now on, the only version of APIRule will be v2.

Summary

In this blog post, I outlined important events and their corresponding necessary actions. I hope this helps you understand what to expect and plan accordingly. If you have any concerns or feedback, leave us a question.

entry.title: Bill subscription in advance more than 3 months
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Our customer sells subscriptions with recurring fee that are usually billed monthly.
Some of their customers sometime request to receive the bills of the first 3 years all together in advance (= 36 periods).
This seems to correspond to the feature "Early billing" but this only allows to have bills created and closed with a maximum offset of 3 months.
I thought of a possible workaround using an allowance but allowance are only linked to usage-based billing, while my scenario mainly concerns recurring invoicing.
Is there any other way to achieve what I described?

Thank you in advance for the help.

entry.title: How to start loop iteration from the beginning after meeting some condition within the loop
entry.content:
Hello,

I need inputs on below scenario:

I have a changing parameter in the method of a Class which is table <ch_table_name> where I would like to process records in this table. The entries in the table are of same type with different data like, every 19 entries, type of data is same but values will be different.

I would like to delete data in this table if it meets the condition. suppose, continuously for couple of times if condition is satisfied.

I will delete these entries and loop iteration HAS to start from beginning to check data again. Is it possible, if Yes, How can I achieve this?

say, 

LOOP <ch_table_name> ASSIGNING <fs_> WHERE <condition>.

IF <cond>.

<logic to check entries and set index lv_start and lv_end to delete data>.

ELSE.

DELETE <ch_table_name> FROM lv_start TO lv_end.

<logic to start iteration from beginning> . ""=> How to handle this?

ENDIF.

ENDLOOP.

Thanks in Advance!!

entry.title: Re: Duplicate Records in Story Reports using Performance...
entry.content:
Hi,

Very common issue when creating performance story reports.
Please note that each section creates a double line. 
See also KBA 3485851. 

In addition when there are multiple owners in a current step this will also create a new line.

BR,


Lesley
